canonical_definition: Across the surveyed literature, the most prevalent definition of image editing is the behavior of modifying an image according to a natural-language instruction. This general framing is specified through a wide variety of sub-tasks, most commonly involving object-level manipulations such as adding, removing, replacing, or altering the color of objects. Other frequently mentioned modifications include changing an image's background or its overall artistic style. A distinct line of work operationalizes this behavior as filling in missing or corrupted parts of an image, a task referred to as "image inpainting," "image infilling," or "generative fill." Less common framings include "drag-based editing," which involves moving content via handle points, and "visual editing," where one paper describes modifying an image by programmatically "drawing boxes, highlighting sections, and masking out areas" to guide a model's attention. To measure performance on this behavior, papers frequently use the MagicBrush benchmark, and also mention using datasets like InstructPix2Pix and DragBench. Image editing is studied as a test of model generalization, and one paper reports that visual editing actions can be used to improve performance on visual reasoning tasks.

definitions.text: Modifying an image according to a natural-language instruction, such as replacing, hiding, or recoloring an object.
definitions.paper: GENOME: Generative Neuro-Symbolic Visual Reasoning by Growing and Reusing Modules

definitions.text: The task of filling in missing or corrupted parts of an image based on a textual instruction.
definitions.paper: PromptFix: You Prompt and We Fix the Photo

definitions.text: The task of adding a new object to an image based on a textual instruction.
definitions.paper: PromptFix: You Prompt and We Fix the Photo

definitions.text: The task of removing a specified object from an image and plausibly filling the background, guided by a textual instruction.
definitions.paper: PromptFix: You Prompt and We Fix the Photo

definitions.text: The task of generating a composite image by integrating a reference object into a background image according to a natural language instruction that specifies the object's location and spatial relationship.
The task of modifying the pose or view of an object within an image while preserving its unique visual identity, often guided by a user-provided mask.
The task of applying the visual identity of a reference object to a different target object already present in a background scene.
The task of substituting an existing object in a background image with a new reference object.
Changing the background of an image to match a target background described in the instruction.
definitions.paper: I2EBench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Instruction-based Image Editing

definitions.text: The observable task of modifying the color of a specified object in an image based on a text instruction.
definitions.paper: I2EBench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Instruction-based Image Editing

definitions.text: The observable task of changing the artistic or visual style of an entire image based on a text instruction.
definitions.paper: I2EBench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Instruction-based Image Editing

definitions.text: Editing an image by moving specified handle points toward target points within an editable region.
definitions.paper: Localize, Understand, Collaborate: Semantic-Aware Dragging via Intention Reasoner

definitions.text: The task of moving or deforming the content within a specified region of an image according to drag points.
definitions.paper: Localize, Understand, Collaborate: Semantic-Aware Dragging via Intention Reasoner

definitions.text: The task of relocating an entire object from a source position to a target position within an image.
definitions.paper: Localize, Understand, Collaborate: Semantic-Aware Dragging via Intention Reasoner

definitions.text: The task of filling in a missing or masked region within an image with plausible, contextually appropriate content.
definitions.paper: Visual Autoregressive Modeling: Scalable Image Generation via Next-Scale Prediction

definitions.text: The task of extending an image beyond its original boundaries by generating new content that is consistent with the existing visual context.
definitions.paper: Visual Autoregressive Modeling: Scalable Image Generation via Next-Scale Prediction

definitions.text: The task of generating pixels to fill in a masked or missing region of an image based on the visible parts.
definitions.paper: Glauber Generative Model: Discrete Diffusion Models via Binary Classification

definitions.text: Completing missing parts of an image based on in-context visual examples.
definitions.paper: Can Generative AI Solve Your In-Context Learning Problem?  A Martingale Perspective

definitions.text: The observable act of modifying an input image through operations such as drawing boxes, highlighting regions, or masking out areas to guide visual attention.
definitions.paper: ReFocus: Visual Editing as a Chain of Thought for Structured Image Understanding

relationships.paper: ReFocus: Visual Editing as a Chain of Thought for Structured Image Understanding
relationships.evidence: We demonstrate that simple visual editing actions generated as Python code from multimodal LLMs–such as drawing boxes, highlighting areas, or masking regions–can significantly improve model performance by directing selective attention. (Section 1)
